chunks.text: Programming Systems (PS) Overview: Many major projects carried out by the group in the past have had a big impact. UNIX. The PASCAL compiler was built here. Split C, a parallel version of C was done here. CCured, secure C, was done here. GPROF: profiling software.
chunks.question: What are some major projects from the Programming Systems group?
chunks.answer: UNIX, PASCAL compiler, Split C, CCured, GPROF.

chunks.text: Topics: Programming Language Design and Implementation: Compiler optimization. Semantics. JIT compilers. Domain-specific languages. Programming Environments and Tools: Monitoring. Programmer search engines. Model-based design. Program Analysis and Verification: Model checking. Static and dynamic analysis. Theorem proving. Schedulability analysis for real-time systems.
chunks.question: What topics are covered in Programming Language Design and Implementation?
chunks.answer: Compiler optimization, semantics, JIT compilers, domain-specific languages.

chunks.text: Software design, synthesis, and testing: Software design for parallel computing; for embedded systems; for numerical computing; for symbolic computing; for distributed computing. Software Testing. Sketching-based synthesis. Research Centers: EPIC Data lab, Sky Computing Lab.
chunks.question: What areas does software design cover in Programming Systems?
chunks.answer: Parallel, embedded, numerical, symbolic, and distributed computing.

chunks.text: Primary Faculty: Sarah Chasins, Alvin Cheung, Armando Fox, Jiantao Jiao, Koushik Sen, Sanjit A. Seshia, Max Willsey, Katherine A. Yelick (coordinator). Secondary Faculty: Aydin Buluç, David E. Culler, Richard J. Fateman, Susan L. Graham, Björn Hartmann, Paul N. Hilfinger, Rishabh Iyer, Edward A. Lee, Michael Lustig, Pierluigi Nuzzo, Dawn Song.
chunks.question: Who are the primary faculty in the Programming Systems area?
chunks.answer: Sarah Chasins, Alvin Cheung, Armando Fox, Jiantao Jiao, Koushik Sen, Sanjit A. Seshia, Max Willsey, Katherine A. Yelick.

chunks.text: Faculty Awards: MacArthur Fellow: Dawn Song, 2010. Okawa Prize: David E. Culler, 2013. National Academy of Engineering (NAE) Member: Katherine A. Yelick, 2017; David E. Culler, 2005; Susan L. Graham, 1993. American Academy of Arts and Sciences Member: Dawn Song, 2025; Katherine A. Yelick, 2017; Susan L. Graham, 1995.
chunks.question: Which faculty members are MacArthur Fellows?

chunks.text: Berkeley Citation: David E. Culler, 2021; Edward A. Lee, 2018; Susan L. Graham, 2009. Sloan Research Fellow: Sarah Chasins, 2026; Alvin Cheung, 2019; Björn Hartmann, 2013; Michael Lustig, 2013; Koushik Sen, 2011; Sanjit A. Seshia, 2008; Dawn Song, 2007.
chunks.question: Who received the Berkeley Citation award?
chunks.answer: David E. Culler, Edward A. Lee, Susan L. Graham.

chunks.text: Related Courses: CS 61A. The Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs, CS 61B. Data Structures, CS 164. Programming Languages and Compilers, CS 169. Software Engineering, CS 263. Design of Programming Languages, CS 264. Implementation of Programming Languages, CS 265. Compiler Optimization and Code Generation, CS C267. Applications of Parallel Computers.
chunks.question: What courses are related to Programming Systems?
